content: checkWarrenConfig (src/diagnostics/checks.ts) emits a single 'warren_config' check that walks every registered project, parsing each .warren/ via the cache when supplied (cache?: WarrenConfigCache) or directly via load() (tests). Empty project list -> ok with 'no projects registered'. WarrenConfigUnavailableError on a project becomes a per-project failure entry rather than throwing past the check; per-file errors are aggregated as '<projectId> <file>: <message>' joined with '; '. Hint always points at /projects/:id/refresh.

checkBurrowPoolReachable (src/diagnostics/checks.ts, warren-c0c9) is the /readyz-side burrow probe — aggregates BurrowClientPool.probe() across every registered worker and degrades to a single `{ok: false, message}` envelope listing failing workers by name. The single-client checkBurrowReachable stays for `warren doctor` (env-derived BurrowClient, not a pool).

checkWarrenDb (src/diagnostics/checks.ts, warren-e2ea) is pure env→DiagnosticCheck: parses WARREN_DB_URL (or synthesizes from legacy WARREN_DB_PATH) via parseDatabaseUrl and reports the dialect on success ('sqlite <path>' or 'postgres'). Surfaces three failures: malformed URL (ValidationError from parseDatabaseUrl), WARREN_DB_URL/WARREN_DB_PATH disagree (sqliteUrlForPath(path) !== url), and the empty sentinel. checkDatabaseReachable (also in checks.ts) is the live SELECT-1 probe: takes an optional AnyWarrenDb (degrades to ok=true 'no db handle wired' when absent so tests can omit the seam), runs pingDatabase(db), and reports 'dialect=sqlite'|'dialect=postgres' on success. Both wired into warren doctor (after the CANOPY_REPO_URL check) and /readyz (as the first probe before the burrow pool fan-out).
